mission.title: Install and Configure Operator Framework on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Operator SDK is a toolkit for building Kubernetes applications, known as Operators, which simplifies the process of managing complex stateful applications on Kubernetes. By using high-level APIs and scaffolding tools, it streamlines Operator development and enhances modularity.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for the Operator SDK to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add operator-sdk https://helm-repository.readthedocs.io/en/latest/repos/stable
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Operator SDK
steps.description:
Install the Operator SDK using Helm, specifying the desired namespace.
```bash
helm install operator-sdk operator-sdk/operator --namespace operator-sdk --create-namespace --version 21.3.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Operator SDK pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-sdk
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Operator SDK deployment.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment operator-sdk -n operator-sdk --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Operator SDK release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall operator-sdk --namespace operator-sdk
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created by the Operator SDK.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep operator-sdk | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the Operator SDK installation.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace operator-sdk
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Operator SDK.
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-sdk -o yaml > operator-sdk-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Operator SDK
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Operator SDK to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade operator-sdk operator-sdk/operator --namespace operator-sdk --version 21.3.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded Operator SDK pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-sdk
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Operator SDK pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n operator-sdk
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with CRDs, verify that they are installed correctly.
```bash
kubectl get crd -n operator-sdk
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being OOMKilled, consider increasing the memory limits set in the deployment.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n operator-sdk
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of the Operator SDK will result in the Operator SDK pods running in the 'operator-sdk' namespace. You can verify this by checking the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n operator-sdk'.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add operator-sdk https://helm-repository.readthedocs.io/en/latest/repos/stable
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and have installed Helm and kubectl.
